---
title: "Penguin Analysis"
subtitle: "From 'Module 3 Report header' in productive-r-workflow.com/"
description: "This document demonstrates the capabilities of Quarto and CSS styling."

author:
  name: "Elisa Allen"
  affiliation: "Imperial College Health Partners"
  email: elisa.allen@imperialcollegehealthpartners.com
  
keywords: "Quarto, CSS styling, data analysis"
date: today

title-block-banner: "#f0f3f5" # Background colour for the title block
title-block-banner-color: "black"  # Font colour for the title block (if not "black", it makes it "white" which doesn't look right)

fig-cap-location: margin
number-sections: true

format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true # collapse code chunks by default
    code-summary: "Show the code"
css: style.css
editor: source
---

## Introduction

Load libraries

```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(patchwork)
library(plotly)
library(DT)
library(knitr)
```

::: callout-important
Remember: to give credit to packages use the `citation()` function.
:::

Source functions.R

```{r}
source(file = "functions.R")
```

Apply dataviz theme

```{r}
theme_set(create_dataviz_theme()) # Dataviz theme
```

## Data loading

The dataset penguins is available online [here](https://allisonhorst.github.io/palmerpenguins/).

```{r}
# Read data using read_excel
data <- read_excel("../input/data.xlsx", na = "NA")
# Tell R how missing values are represented in the XLSX spreadsheet
```

![How bill lenght and depth are measured](assets/culmen_depth.png){fig-align="center" width="300"}

Display the penguin dataset using the `DT` package.

```{r}
datatable(data, filter = "top")
```

Summarise the dataset.

```{r}
# Summary
summary(data)
```

## Descriptive analytics

Average bill length for **Adelie** penguins.

```{r, fig.width=6, fig.height=6, fig.align='center'}
# Calculating mean bill length for "Adelie" and islands using dplyr - but these means are not being stored, just for illustration
avg_bill_length_Adelie <- data %>%
  filter(species == "Adelie") %>%
  group_by(island) %>%
  summarize(mean_bill_length = num(mean(bill_length_mm, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 2))

kable(avg_bill_length_Adelie)
```

Use the `layout-ncol` to create a 2 column layout.

```{r}
#| layout-ncol: 2

data %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarize(average_bill_length = num(mean(bill_length_mm, na.rm = TRUE)))

data %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarize(average_bill_depth = num(mean(bill_depth_mm, na.rm = TRUE)))
```

```{r}
### Plot using ggplot2 ---
p <- data %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm, color = species, shape = species)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Bill Length (mm)", y = "Bill Depth (mm)", title = "Penguin Bill Dimensions") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Adelie" = 16, "Chinstrap" = 17, "Gentoo" = 18))

ggplotly(p)

### Call CALC_MEAN_BILL function 3 times, once for each island for species="Adelie"
avg_Torgersen <- calc_mean_bill("Torgersen")
avg_Biscoe <- calc_mean_bill("Biscoe")
avg_Dream <- calc_mean_bill("Dream")
```

The average bill length for Adelie penguins from **Torgersen island** is `r avg_Torgersen`, calculated as $\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}x_i$, where $x_i$ is the bill length for the $i$ penguin in the group.

The average bill length for Adelie penguins from **Biscoe island** is `r avg_Biscoe`.

The average bill length for Adelie penguins from **Dream island** is `r avg_Dream`.

Use `patchwork` to make 3 side-by-side scatterplots using the `create_scatterplot()` function.

```{r}
### Call CREATE_SCATTERPLOT function to shows the relationship between bill length and bill depth with a scatterplot ---
p1 <- create_scatterplot(data, "Adelie", ) # data, species, island
p2 <- create_scatterplot(data, "Chinstrap", ) # data, species, island
p3 <- create_scatterplot(data, "Gentoo", ) # data, species, island

p1 + p2 + p3
```

::: callout-important
The `create_scatterplot` function automatically assigns the color to each `species`.

To manuallly specify the color, use `scale_color_manual(values = c("Adelie" = "#FF8C00", "Chinstrap" = "#9932CC", "Gentoo" = "#008B8B"))` after `geom_point()`.
:::
